seo_title: Trump Admin Permits Volvo to Keep Selling Connected Cars in the U.S.
meta_description: The Trump administration has given Volvo permission to continue selling connected cars in the US, despite concerns over data privacy and security.
body_html:
The Trump administration has given Volvo permission to continue selling connected cars in the US. This decision comes despite concerns over data privacy and security.

Key Expansion PlansVolvo can now move forward with its expansion plans for its U.S. factoryGeely Holdings, Volvo's majority owner, will continue to support the company's growth in the US marketConnected Car Sales in the USCountrySales DataUSConnected car sales to continue despite Trump admin's decisionIndustry Impact and ConcernsConcerns over data privacy and security have been raised by some experts regarding the sale of connected cars in the US.

source.title: Trump Admin permits Volvo to keep selling connected cars in the U.S.
source.summary: The Trump administration has allowed Volvo to continue selling connected cars in the United States. Volvo, a majority-owned subsidiary of China's Geely Holdings, will be able to proceed with its expansion plans for its US factory. This decision likely stems from the fact that Volvo is owned by a Chinese company, and the US-China trade tensions may have influenced this outcome.
